channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How philosopher Shannon Vallor delivered the year’s best critique of AI
item.description: A few years ago, Shannon Vallor found herself in front of Cloud Gate, Anish Kapoor’s hulking mercury drop of a sculpture, better known as the Bean, in Chicago’s Millennium Park. Staring into its shiny mirrored surface, she noticed something.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Google wants the FTC to stop exclusive Microsoft cloud deal with OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft is OpenAI’s exclusive cloud provider, but Google says this could be costly and damaging to customers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GM shuts down robotaxi project to focus on autonomous vehicles
item.description: General Motors (GM) said Tuesday that it will no longer fund the development of robotaxis and instead focus on creating fully autonomous personal vehicles. Cruise, GM’s self-driving car subsidiary, will be combined with the automaker’s own team to form a single unit focused on autonomous driving.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: How to catch the Geminids, one of the strongest meteor showers of the year
item.description: The Geminids are among the few major meteor showers to come from asteroids, and this year they will peak on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Solar company leverages digital transformation for a brighter tomorrow
item.description: Humans have long been fascinated by the power of the sun. Since as early as the 7th Century B.C., humans used magnifying glass to spark fires with sunlight. Later, we used mirrors to light torches.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: TikTok files challenge against Canadian government order to dissolve its business in the country
item.description: TikTok has challenged a Canadian government order to shut down the Chinese video-sharing app’s business operations in the country that was imposed over national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The future of data: A 5-pillar approach to modern data management
item.description: In today’s economy, as the saying goes, data is the new gold — a valuable asset from a financial standpoint. However, from a company’s existential perspective, there’s an even more fitting analogy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft to launch zero water consumption cooling for future data centers
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new design for data centers to optimize artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, implementing a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NDAA to give DoD components more flexibility to procure cyber products
item.description: A provision that made it into the compromise legislation will give the Defense Department components more authority to purchase alternative cyber products. The post NDAA to give DoD components more flexibility to procure cyber products first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nokia expands data center SONiC support
item.description: Nokia is offering customers an alternative way to set up data center networks by expanding its data center fabric package to support the open-source Software for Open Networking in the Cloud (SONiC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI releases video creation tool
item.description: ChatGPT producer OpenAI rolled out a video creation tool this week that lets paying customers create short-length video clips through text prompts. OpenAI introduced Sora, a video generation model, on Monday. It can generate realistic videos in a resolution up to 1080p and up to 20 seconds long.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI releases AI video generator Sora but limits how it depicts people
item.description: OpenAI has publicly released its new artificial intelligence video generator Sora but won’t let most users depict people as it monitors for patterns of misuse.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Unlocking AI potential: The essential role of hybrid, multi-cloud strategies
item.description: At NetApp, we recognize that AI is not merely a technological tool—it’s a transformative mindset that can reshape organizations and industries. To harness its full potential, it is essential to cultivate a data-driven culture that permeates every level of your company.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows iPhone users how to easily delete multiple emails and manage their inboxes to reduce digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Best Places to Work in IT 2025
item.description: What makes a company a great place to work for IT professionals? Top salaries and benefits certainly help, but those are just table stakes. Tech workers are looking for challenging projects, growth opportunities, and continuous learning in a supportive workplace.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Social media influencer is a growing career choice. Colleges have noticed
item.description: Have influencers on social media created a new career field worthy of formal education? A growing number of universities are looking at classes and majors aimed at giving influencers the skills needed to spot misinformation, communicate effectively with audiences and understand business contracts t
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Total Quality Logistics: Building a ‘talent destination’ through employee engagement
item.description: Don’t talk about industry slowdowns to Total Quality Logistics (TQL), which has made its largest-ever technology investments over the past three years to bring greater agility and resilience to the business.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI expands multimodal capabilities with updated text-to-video model
item.description: OpenAI has released a new version of its text-to-video AI model, Sora, for ChatGPT Plus and Pro users, marking another step in expansion into multimodal AI technologies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cloudflare Radar Year in Review 2024: big source of traffic is AI crawlers
item.description: The internet is increasingly where we live today. In fact, global internet traffic grew 17.2% this year alone, according to Cloudflare.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple’s iPhone SE 4 will matter very much indeed
item.description: It might not be the biggest-selling or most expensive product in Apple’s basket, but a very important part of Apple’s future will be defined by the upcoming iPhone SE upgrade in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX
item.description: Michelyne LeBlanc, the deputy CIO of the Defense Commissary Agency, said the move to zero trust is all about ensuring customer confidence in its services. The post Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Low-tech solutions to high-tech cybercrimes
item.description: You might hear that 2025 will be the Year of artificial intelligence (AI) cybercrime. But the trend really began in 2024. AI crime will prove so overwhelming that some say the only way to fight it is through AI security software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says at least 8 US telecom firms, dozens of nations impacted by China hacking campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, ABC 4
